preview_text:
The model favors Algeria at 45%, with Jordan at 25% and a draw at 30%. Algeria enters as the clear statistical favorite, but the model assigns Jordan roughly a quarter chance to upset, suggesting a competitive match rather than a foregone conclusion.

The 30% draw probability signals meaningful uncertainty about who will prevail inside ninety minutes. The win probabilities span 20 percentage points, indicating Algeria is favored but not overwhelmingly so. A draw outcome ranks second-most likely, reflecting the competitive gap between the two teams.

The model and market diverge notably. The sportsbook consensus places Algeria at 62%, roughly 17 percentage points higher than the model's 45%, and prices Jordan at only 15%—half the model's 25%. Polymarket prices align closely with sportsbook consensus on the same spread. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is correct; the gap reflects material disagreement between statistical and market methodologies.
